Note: See how you translated **Levites** in [2:40](../02/40.md). Review the explanation in the note there if that would be helpful.

Quote: Jozabad, and Shimei, and Kelaiah (that is, Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer
Note: These are the names of six men and an alternate name for one of them.

Quote: the singers
Note: The term **singers** refers to the same occupational group as in [2:41](../02/41.md). Review the explanation in the note there if that would be helpful.

Quote: Eliashib
Note: **Eliashib** is the name of a man.

Quote: the gatekeepers
Note: The term **gatekeepers** refers to the same occupational group as in [2:42](../02/42.md). Review the explanation in the note there if that would be helpful.

Quote: Shallum, and Telem, and Uri
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: And these were from Israel
Note: All of the men in the lists in this chapter were Israelites. So the phrase **from Israel** refers to the other Israelite men who did not belong to the clans or groups mentioned in the previous lists. If it would be helpful for your readers, you could make this clear in your translation. Alternate translation: “These were from the rest of the Israelites”

Quote: From the sons of Parosh
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Parosh”

Quote: Ramiah, and Izziah, and Malkijah, and Mijamin, and Eleazar, and Malkijah, and Benaiah
Note: These are the names of seven men.

Quote: And from the sons of Elam
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Elam”

Quote: Mattaniah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, and Abdi, and Jeremoth, and Elijah
Note: These are the names of six men.

Quote: And from the sons of Zattu
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Zattu”

Quote: Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, and Jeremoth, and Zabad, and Aziza
Note: These are the names of six men.

Quote: And from the sons of Bebai
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “from the descendants of Bebai”

Quote: Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, and Athlai
Note: These are the names of four men.

Quote: And from the sons of Bani
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Bani”

Quote: Meshullam, Malluk, and Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal, Jeremoth
Note: These are the names of six men.

Quote: And from the sons of Pahath-Moab
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Pahath-Moab”

Quote: Adna, and Kelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezalel, and Binnui, and Manasseh
Note: These are the names of eight men.

Quote: And from the sons of Harim
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Harim”

Quote: Eliezer, Ishijah, Malkijah, Shemaiah, Shimeon
Note: These are the names of five men.

Quote: Benjamin, Malluk, Shemariah
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: From the sons of Hashum
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Hashum”

Quote: Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, and Shimei
Note: These are the names of eight men.

Quote: From the sons of Bani
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Bani”

Quote: Maadai, Amram, and Uel
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Benaiah, Bedeiah, Keluhi
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasu
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: and Bani, and Binnui, Shimei
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: and Bani, and Binnui, Shimei
Note: The ULT represents the reading of the Hebrew text, but many scholars believe that the correct reading of this verse is found in ancient Greek translations. This is represented in the UST. You may choose to follow the reading of the preferred Bible translation used in your area, and include the other reading in a footnote.

Quote: and Shelemiah, and Nathan, and Adaiah
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Maknadebai, Shashai, Sharai
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Azarel, and Shelemiah, Shemariah
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: Shallum, Amariah, Joseph
Note: These are the names of three men.

Quote: From the sons of Nebo
Note: Here, **sons** means “descendants.” Alternate translation: “From the descendants of Nebo”

Quote: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, and Joel, Benaiah
Note: These are the names of seven men.

Quote: All of these had lifted foreign women
Note: As in [9:2](../09/02.md), **lifted** is an idiom that means “married.” Alternate translation: “All of these men had married foreign women”

Quote: And there were women among them, and they bore sons
Note: The implication is that if a foreign wife had borne children, the Israelite husband would send both the wife and her children away, as [10:3](../10/03.md) describes. If it would be helpful to your readers, you could say that explicitly. Alternate translation: “Some of the foreign wives had borne children, and the men who had married them divorced them and sent away both them and their children.”

Quote: sons
Note: The term **sons** here means “children” and includes both boys and girls. Alternate translation: “children”
